questions.text: Which whorl of a flower is usually green and protects the bud?
questions.explanation: The calyx is made of sepals which protect the developing flower bud.

questions.text: Which part of a leaf is involved in gas exchange?
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores that allow exchange of gases in the leaf.

questions.text: Which stem modification is used for storage of water?
questions.explanation: Succulent stems store water in arid conditions.

questions.text: Which root type is characteristic of monocots?
questions.explanation: Monocots usually have a fibrous root system.

questions.text: The arrangement of veins in a leaf is called:
questions.explanation: Venation refers to the pattern of veins in the leaf blade.

questions.text: A flower with sepals and petals in separate whorls is called:
questions.explanation: A complete flower has all four whorls: calyx, corolla, androecium, and gynoecium.

questions.text: In dicotyledonous plants, the direct elongation of the radicle leads to the formation of:
questions.explanation: Primary root grows inside the soil and bears lateral roots.

questions.text: Roots arising from parts of the plant other than the radicle are called:
questions.explanation: Examples include grass, Monstera and the banyan tree.

questions.text: The region of the root responsible for the growth of the root in length is:
questions.explanation: Cells in this region undergo rapid elongation and enlargement.

questions.text: Pneumatophores are special roots that help in:
questions.explanation: They are found in plants growing in swampy areas like Rhizophora.

questions.text: The stem develops from the ________ of the embryo of a germinating seed.
questions.explanation: The stem is the ascending part of the axis bearing branches, leaves, flowers and fruits.

questions.text: In some plants like Opuntia, the stem is modified into a flattened structure to perform:
questions.explanation: Flattened (Opuntia) or fleshy cylindrical (Euphorbia) stems contain chlorophyll and carry out photosynthesis.

questions.text: The point on the stem where leaves are attached is called:
questions.explanation: Internodes are the portions between two nodes.

questions.text: A typical leaf consists of three main parts:
questions.explanation: Leaf is attached to the stem by the leaf base.

questions.text: When the veinlets form a network, the venation is termed as:
questions.explanation: Leaves of dicotyledonous plants generally possess reticulate venation.

questions.text: The arrangement of leaves on the stem or branch is called:
questions.explanation: This is usually of three types: alternate, opposite and whorled.

questions.text: In a compound leaf, if the leaflets are attached at a common point, i.e., at the tip of petiole, it is:
questions.explanation: Example: Silk cotton.

questions.text: The arrangement of flowers on the floral axis is termed as:
questions.explanation: Depending on whether the apex gets converted into a flower or continues to grow, racemose and cymose types are defined.

questions.text: In racemose type of inflorescence, the main axis:
questions.explanation: The flowers are borne laterally in an acropetal succession.

questions.text: When a flower can be divided into two equal radial halves in any radial plane passing through the centre, it is said to be:
questions.explanation: Examples: mustard, datura, chilli.

questions.text: A flower having both androecium and gynoecium is:
questions.explanation: Most flowering plants have bisexual flowers.

questions.text: If the gynoecium occupies the highest position while the other parts are situated below it, the flower is:
questions.explanation: The ovary in such flowers is said to be superior.

questions.text: The mode of arrangement of sepals or petals in floral bud with respect to the other members of the same whorl is known as:
questions.explanation: Types include valvate, twisted, imbricate and vexillary.

questions.text: A sterile stamen is called:
questions.explanation: Staminodes do not produce pollen grains.

questions.text: When stamens are attached to the petals, they are:
questions.explanation: Example: Brinjal.

questions.text: The arrangement of ovules within the ovary is known as:
questions.explanation: Types include marginal, axile, parietal, basal, central and free central.

questions.text: The fruit is a characteristic feature of:
questions.explanation: It is a mature or ripened ovary, developed after fertilization.

questions.text: A fruit developed without fertilization of the ovary is called:
questions.explanation: Example: Banana.

questions.text: In mango and coconut, the fruit is known as a:
questions.explanation: They develop from monocarpellary superior ovaries and are one seeded.

questions.text: The outer covering of a seed is the:
questions.explanation: The seed coat has two layers: the outer testa and the inner tegmen.

questions.text: In monocotyledonous seeds like maize, the outer covering of endosperm separates the embryo by a proteinous layer called:
questions.explanation: The aleurone layer is rich in proteins.

questions.text: The large, shield-shaped cotyledon in monocots is known as:
questions.explanation: It is characteristic of grass family seeds.

questions.text: The plumule and radicle in monocots are enclosed in sheaths called:
questions.explanation: Coleoptile encloses plumule and coleorhiza encloses radicle.

questions.text: The scars on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit is called:
questions.explanation: Hilum is a point of attachment.

questions.text: Which of the following is a floral formula for Fabaceae?
questions.explanation: Fabaceae flowers are zygomorphic (%) and have diadelphous stamens (9)+1.

questions.text: Family Solanaceae is commonly known as the:
questions.explanation: It is a large family widely distributed in tropics and subtropics.

questions.text: In Solanaceae, the stamens are:
questions.explanation: Stamens are attached to the petals.

questions.text: The family Liliaceae is characteristic of:
questions.explanation: It is also known as the Lily family.

questions.text: In Liliaceae, the perianth members are often united into a tube and exhibit ________ aestivation.
questions.explanation: Tepals show valvate aestivation.

questions.text: Colchicine is obtained from a plant belonging to:
questions.explanation: Colchicum autumnale is a member of Liliaceae.
